title: DeepSeek-V4-Pro
description: The larger DeepSeek-V4 reasoning model, built around long-context attention, MoE routing, and coupled post-training and serving choices.
openingSummary: DeepSeek-V4-Pro is useful to study because the model page points to a whole stack, not just a bigger checkpoint. Its identity comes from how V4-specific modules are combined with earlier attention and post-training ideas.

whatItIs.title: What It Is
whatItIs.body: DeepSeek-V4-Pro is the larger reasoning-oriented model in the DeepSeek-V4 release. It is a text model with a transformer-style backbone, sparse expert blocks, and an unusually large advertised context length.

inputsAndOutputs.title: Inputs And Outputs
inputsAndOutputs.body: The model takes text tokens and produces text tokens. The practical difference from an ordinary chat model is not modality. It is that the stack is designed to preserve useful work over very long prompts and long reasoning traces.

architecture.title: Architecture
architecture.body: The model keeps a transformer skeleton, but the paper highlights several different layers of lineage instead of one single signature block. Manifold-constrained hyper-connections plus the compressed sparse attention (CSA) and heavily compressed attention (HCA) long-context variants are presented as V4-specific additions, while MLA-style cache compression builds on the earlier DeepSeek-V2 multi-head latent attention design.

importantModules.title: Important Modules
importantModules.body: These are the modules worth opening if you want to understand why the V4 stack behaves differently from a plain decoder-only transformer.

training.title: Training
training.body: The public story emphasizes specialist post-training, on-policy distillation, and FP4 quantization-aware work. The important lineage point is that on-policy distillation predates V4, while this release shows how DeepSeek uses that earlier method alongside newer V4-specific post-training and deployment work.

practicalNotes.title: Practical Notes
practicalNotes.body: The million-token context is the headline, but the page is easiest to read as a coordinated stack. Architecture choices reduce long-context cost, and serving choices keep that cost from collapsing the runtime.

architectureGraph.alt: DeepSeek-V4-Pro architecture diagram with token embeddings, RoPE position encoding, a repeated decoder block containing compressed attention, RMSNorm, and DeepSeekMoE, then an output projection and softmax.
architectureGraph.caption: DeepSeek-V4-Pro keeps the GPT-style decoder stack shape, but swaps in RoPE, compressed long-context attention, RMSNorm, and DeepSeekMoE inside each repeated block.

outputProbabilities.label:
Output
Probabilities
outputProbabilities.summary: The model's probability distribution over the next token

softmax.label: Softmax
softmax.summary: Turns final vocabulary scores into probabilities

linear.label:
Output
Linear
linear.summary: Projects the final hidden state into vocabulary logits

decoderStack.summary: Repeated decoder block container for the main DeepSeek-V4 stack

repeatMarker.label: N×
repeatMarker.summary: This decoder block repeats through the full stack

rmsnormAttention.label: RMSNorm
rmsnormAttention.summary: Normalizes the residual stream before the long-context attention step

compressedAttention.label:
Compressed
Attention
(CSA + HCA)
compressedAttention.summary: The attention path combines compressed sparse and heavily compressed attention for very long context

rmsnormMoe.label: RMSNorm
rmsnormMoe.summary: Normalizes the stream before the sparse expert block

deepseekmoe.label: DeepSeekMoE
deepseekmoe.summary: Routes each token through a small active set of experts instead of one dense feed-forward path

ropeInject.summary: Applies token states together with rotary position information before the stack

rope.label: RoPE
rope.summary: Rotary position encoding gives the attention stack token order information

inputEmbedding.label:
Input
Embedding
inputEmbedding.summary: Maps token IDs into hidden vectors
